input.text:
End of shift 1 handoff: 

---
Patient 1 (Bed 1): Mrs. Patricia Reyes, 84, post-fall hip ORIF day 4. Allergies: penicillin. Full code. Vitals stable: HR 78, RR 16, SpO2 97%, BP 132, Temp 36.8°C (NEWS2: 0). Receiving scheduled enoxaparin 40 mg SC—administered during shift. No acute issues identified during shift.

Patient 2 (Bed 2, FOCAL): Mrs. Elena Aquino, 78, post-CAP day 6. Allergies: sulfa. DNR/POLST. Comorbid CHF, T2DM (on furosemide, metformin). Received azithromycin (last dose today), furosemide, and metformin. Vitals: HR 89, RR 18, SpO2 95%, BP 124, Temp 37.1°C (NEWS2: 1). Ambient observation: Today, Mrs. Aquino is paler, more withdrawn than in prior shifts, and has decreased appetite (seen picking at breakfast). No overt respiratory distress or change in mental status, but these subtle shifts are new. Recommend close monitoring for further behavioral or clinical change.

Patient 3 (Bed 3): Mr. Walter Goldberg, 91, dementia and recurrent UTI. Allergies: none. DNR/DNI/DNH/POLST. NEWS2: 6 (HR 90, RR 20, SpO2 93%, BP 110, Temp 37.6°C). Received scheduled ceftriaxone 1g IV. No acute change from baseline, continue to monitor.

---
FOCUS for incoming shift: Mrs. Aquino (P2) shows subtle decline in energy and interest—watch closely for further changes. All meds and observations up to date.

input.text:
End of Shift 1 Handoff – RN1

Bed 1: Mrs. Patricia Reyes, 84
• Status: Post-hip ORIF day 4. Allergic to penicillin. Full code.
• Vitals: HR 78, RR 16, SpO2 97%, BP 132, Temp 36.8°C (NEWS2: 0).
• Interventions: Enoxaparin 40 mg SC given. Stable. No acute concerns.

Bed 2: Mrs. Elena Aquino, 78 (FOCAL)
• Status: Post-CAP day 6. DNR/POLST. Allergic to sulfa. History: CHF, T2DM (on furosemide, metformin).
• Meds: Last dose azithromycin 250 mg PO given today; furosemide 20 mg PO and metformin 500 mg PO given.
• Vitals: HR 89, RR 18, SpO2 95%, BP 124, Temp 37.1°C (NEWS2: 1).
• Ambient/behavioral: Mrs. Aquino appears more withdrawn and pale compared to yesterday, with decreased appetite (picking at breakfast). No clear respiratory distress or acute change in mental status, but these subtle changes are new. Recommend vigilant monitoring for clinical or behavioral worsening.

Bed 3: Mr. Walter Goldberg, 91
• Status: Recurrent UTI, moderate dementia. DNR/DNI/DNH/POLST. No allergies.
• Vitals: HR 90, RR 20, SpO2 93%, BP 110, Temp 37.6°C (NEWS2: 6).
• Ceftriaxone 1g IV given. No acute changes from baseline noted this shift.

FOCUS: Watch Mrs. Aquino (Bed 2) for evolving new/worsening symptoms. Subtle behavioral change may precede vital sign shift. All interventions to this point are complete and charted. Allergies and code statuses up to date.
